1.title: 'God save king Solomon!'
1.text: Solomon was anointed with the oil from the tabernacle, to the sound of the trumpet, and the people shouted: 'God save king Solomon!' — confirming David's succession.

1.question: How is David described at the start of 1 Kings?
Who exalted himself, declaring himself king without his father's knowledge?
Who warned David of Adonijah's attempt to seize the throne?
What had David sworn to Bath-sheba about the successor?
options: That Solomon would reign after him
That he would divide the kingdom
That there would be no king

1.question: Whom did David have anointed king in his place?
Where was Solomon anointed king?
options: At Gihon, by Zadok and Nathan

1.question: How did the people react to Solomon's anointing?
What happened to Adonijah's guests when they heard about Solomon?
What did Adonijah do in fear of Solomon?
options: He caught hold of the horns of the altar

1.question: What did Solomon do with Adonijah at that moment?
options: He spared him, on condition he be faithful
He had him killed at once

1.question: What did Solomon say to Adonijah in sparing him?
options: To go home, if he would show himself a worthy man
That he would rule with him

2.title: David's last charge
2.text: Before dying, David charged Solomon: 'be thou strong, and show thyself a man; keep the charge of the Lord' — leaving him a spiritual legacy, not just a political one.

2.question: What central charge did David give Solomon before dying?
options: To keep the commandments of the Lord and be strong

2.question: What did David charge Solomon concerning Joab and Shimei?
How did David end his days?
options: He died in peace and was buried in the city of David

2.question: What did Adonijah ask for, which cost him his life?
What did Solomon do to Adonijah over that request?
options: He had him killed, seeing in it a threat to the throne

2.question: What did Solomon do to the priest Abiathar?
options: He deposed him and banished him to Anathoth

2.question: How did Joab die?
options: He was killed by the altar, by Solomon's order

2.question: Who replaced Joab over the army?
What condition did Solomon impose on Shimei?
options: That he not leave Jerusalem, on pain of death
That he serve in the temple

2.question: What happened to Shimei when he disobeyed and left Jerusalem?

3.title: The request that pleased God
3.text: Solomon could have asked for riches or long life, but he asked for 'an understanding heart to judge the people' — so God gave him also what he did not ask.

3.question: How did Solomon's reign begin in his relationship with God?
options: He loved the Lord, but still sacrificed in the high places

3.question: What did God offer Solomon in a dream at Gibeon?
What did Solomon ask of God?
options: Wisdom to govern and discern good and evil

3.question: What did God give Solomon, besides what he asked?
How did Solomon reveal his wisdom in the case of the two women?
options: He proposed dividing the baby, revealing the true mother

4.title: 'Every man under his vine'
4.text: At the height of Solomon's peace, it was said every Israelite lived secure 'under his vine and under his fig tree' — a biblical image of prosperity and peace.

4.question: What does chapter 4 list at the start?
How did Solomon arrange provision for the court?
options: Twelve deputies, each providing one month a year

4.question: What was life like in Judah and Israel under Solomon?
options: Prosperous and secure, 'every man under his vine'

4.question: What gift marked Solomon's reign?
options: An immense wisdom, given by God

4.question: How does the Bible measure Solomon's wisdom?
options: Greater than that of all the wise men of the East

4.question: What did Solomon produce, showing his wisdom?
Who came to hear Solomon's wisdom?
options: People of all nations and kings of the earth

5.title: Why it fell to Solomon
5.text: David could not build the temple, having shed much blood in war; in a time of peace, it fell to his son Solomon to raise the house of the Lord.

5.question: With whom did Solomon make an alliance to obtain timber for the temple?
What did Hiram supply for building the temple?
What did Solomon give Hiram in exchange for the timber?
options: Wheat and oil for his household

5.question: How did Solomon gather a workforce for the great work?
options: He used only the royal family

6.title: A temple raised in silence
6.text: No sound of hammer, axe or tool was heard at the temple during construction: the stones were prepared offsite — a sign of reverence in raising the house of God.

6.question: When did Solomon begin to build the temple?
options: In the fourth year of his reign
At the end of his life

6.question: What was notable about the stones during construction?
options: They were prepared offsite; no hammer was heard at the temple
They were cut on-site with great noise

6.question: What promise did God make Solomon about the temple?
options: He would dwell with Israel if he kept His commandments
That it would last forever unconditionally
That He would double his riches
That no one would attack it

6.question: With what was the interior of the temple lined?
What was in the inner sanctuary (the Holy of Holies)?
options: Two wooden cherubim overlaid with gold

6.question: What were the temple's walls and doors like?
options: Carved with cherubim, palm trees and flowers, and overlaid with gold

6.question: How long did building the temple take?

7.title: Jachin and Boaz
7.text: The two bronze pillars of the porch had meaningful names: Jachin ('he establishes') and Boaz ('in him is strength') — a reminder that the house rested on God.

7.question: What did Solomon build, besides the temple?
options: His own palace, over thirteen years

7.question: Who was Hiram, called from Tyre for the work?
What were the names of the two bronze pillars of the temple porch?
What great bronze piece did Hiram make, resting on twelve oxen?
What was the bronze 'sea' for in the temple?
What other bronze pieces were made for the temple?
Of what material were the temple's sacred vessels?
Who made all these metal works for the temple?
What did Solomon store when the work was finished?
What does chapter 7 describe in detail?
options: The building of the palace and the temple's furnishings

8.title: The glory that filled the temple
8.text: When the priests came out, a cloud filled the temple: the glory of the Lord was so intense they could not stand to minister. God had accepted the house.

8.question: What did the priests bring into the Holy of Holies at the dedication?
What was inside the ark?
options: The two stone tablets of Moses

8.question: What filled the temple when the priests came out?
options: A cloud — the glory of the Lord

8.question: What did Solomon recall as he blessed the people?
options: The promise made to David, now fulfilled

8.question: What question did Solomon ask in his prayer?
options: 'Will God dwell on the earth?', for the heavens cannot contain Him

8.question: What did Solomon ask about prayers made toward the temple?
options: That God hear them from heaven and forgive

8.question: What did Solomon ask for times of drought and famine?
options: That God hear and forgive the repentant people

8.question: For whom else did Solomon pray, besides Israel?
options: For the foreigner who would come to pray at the temple

8.question: What did Solomon ask for when the people sinned and were carried captive?
options: That, repenting, God hear and forgive them

8.question: How did Solomon describe Israel before God in the prayer?
options: As an inheritance God brought out of Egypt

8.question: What did Solomon do when he finished the long prayer?
options: He blessed all the congregation of Israel
He offered himself as a priest

8.question: What did Solomon ask the people's heart to be?
options: Wholly the Lord's, keeping His commandments

8.question: How did the dedication of the temple end?
options: With many sacrifices and a great feast

9.title: 'Mine eyes shall be there'
9.text: God said He had hallowed the temple and that 'mine eyes and mine heart shall be there perpetually' — but this depended on the faithfulness of Solomon and the people.

9.question: What did the Lord do after Solomon finished the temple?
options: He appeared to him again, accepting the house
He told him to build another

9.question: What warning did God give Solomon?
options: That if they turned to other gods, Israel and the temple would be destroyed
That he would live a thousand years
That He would double his riches
That he would never be attacked

9.question: What did Solomon give Hiram, which displeased him?
How did Solomon carry out his great building projects?
options: With forced labor of non-Israelite peoples

9.question: What did Solomon's fleet bring from Ophir?

10.title: The queen of the South
10.text: Jesus would cite 'the queen of the South,' who came from the ends of the earth to hear Solomon's wisdom, as an example of one who seeks truth wholeheartedly (Matthew 12).

10.question: Who came to test Solomon with hard questions?
How did the Queen of Sheba react on seeing Solomon's wisdom and wealth?
options: She was overwhelmed: 'the half was not told me'

10.question: What did the Queen of Sheba give Solomon?
What was silver like in Solomon's days, it was so abundant?
options: It was like stones, of little special value

11.title: Even the wise can fall
11.text: Solomon, the wisest man, turned away by compromising with the idolatry of his foreign wives — a warning that wisdom without faithfulness is not enough.

11.question: What turned Solomon's heart away in his old age?
options: His many foreign wives and their gods

11.question: What did Solomon go so far as to do, in turning away?
options: He built high places for pagan gods

11.question: What did the Lord decide because of Solomon's unfaithfulness?
options: To rend the kingdom, but only after his death

11.question: For whose sake would God leave one tribe to Solomon's house?
options: For the sake of David and Jerusalem

11.question: What adversaries did God raise up against Solomon?
What sign did the prophet Ahijah give Jeroboam?
options: He tore a garment into twelve pieces and gave him ten

11.question: What did Ahijah's prophecy mean?
options: That Jeroboam would reign over ten tribes of Israel

11.question: What happened at the end of Solomon's reign?
options: He died and his son Rehoboam reigned in his place

12.title: The 'sin of Jeroboam'
12.text: So the north wouldn't go up to Jerusalem, Jeroboam made two golden calves (at Beth-el and Dan). This idolatry, the 'sin of Jeroboam,' condemns king after king in the book.

12.question: Where did Rehoboam go to be made king?
What did the people ask of Rehoboam?
What counsel did Rehoboam follow, angering the people?
What did Israel do in the face of Rehoboam's harshness?
options: It rebelled: 'To your tents, O Israel!'
It crowned another son of Solomon

12.question: How was the kingdom divided?
options: Ten tribes with Jeroboam; Judah and Benjamin with Rehoboam

12.question: What did Jeroboam do so the north would not worship in Jerusalem?
options: He made two golden calves, at Beth-el and Dan

12.question: What else did Jeroboam institute, leading the people astray?
options: His own priests and feasts, outside the Law

13.title: Obeying the word of God
13.text: The man of God performed mighty signs, yet died for disobeying a clear command of the Lord. The chapter teaches that neither gifts nor miracles replace obedience.

13.question: What did the man of God prophesy against at Beth-el?
What happened to Jeroboam's hand when he stretched it out against the prophet?
options: It dried up, then was restored

13.question: What had the man of God been commanded not to do?
options: Not eat or drink there, nor return by the same way

13.question: Who deceived the man of God into returning to eat?
What happened to the man of God for disobeying?
options: He was killed by a lion on the way

13.question: What did the lion do that was unusual beside the body?
options: It stood by, not devouring the body or the donkey

13.question: What did Jeroboam do after this sign?
options: He did not turn from his evil way

14.title: The measure of the northern kings
14.text: From here on, nearly every king of Israel is judged by walking 'in the sin of Jeroboam' — the calf idolatry that split the worship of God.

14.question: Why did Jeroboam's wife go disguised to the prophet Ahijah?
options: To learn what would happen to their sick son

14.question: What did the blind Ahijah prophesy to Jeroboam's house?
options: That it would be destroyed because of its idolatry
That it would reign a thousand years

14.question: What would happen to Jeroboam's sick son?
options: He would die as soon as his mother entered the city

14.question: What in fact happened to the son, fulfilling the prophecy?
options: He died when his mother arrived, and all Israel mourned him

14.question: What was Rehoboam's reign like in Judah?
options: He did evil, with high places and idols

14.question: Who invaded Judah and plundered the temple in Rehoboam's time?
What did Rehoboam make in place of the gold shields taken away?

15.title: Asa, a whole heart
15.text: Among so many wicked kings, Asa of Judah stands out: he removed the idols and his heart was 'perfect toward the Lord' all his life — a rare good example.

15.question: Who was Asa, king of Judah, in this chapter?
options: A king who did right and removed idols

15.question: What was Asa's heart like toward the Lord?
With whom did Asa make a league against Baasha of Israel?
Who reigned in Israel after Jeroboam, doing evil?
Who killed Nadab and destroyed Jeroboam's house?
What did the destruction of Jeroboam's house fulfill?

16.title: Ahab and Jezebel
16.text: Ahab was worse than all the kings before him: he married Jezebel, princess of Sidon, and established Baal worship in Israel — which will provoke Elijah's ministry.

16.question: What pattern recurs among the kings of Israel in this chapter?
options: They do evil, following the sin of Jeroboam

16.question: Who killed Elah and seized the throne for only seven days?
What did Zimri do when he saw himself surrounded and lost?
options: He burned the palace down over himself

16.question: Who became king and founded the city of Samaria?
How does the chapter describe Ahab, son of Omri?
options: He did evil more than all the kings before him

16.question: Whom did Ahab marry, deepening the idolatry?
options: Jezebel, and he came to serve Baal

16.question: What did Ahab build for Baal in Samaria?

17.title: Elijah against Baal
17.text: The drought Elijah announced was a direct blow against Baal, worshiped as the god of rain and fertility: the Lord would show who really governs the sky and life.

17.question: What did Elijah announce to king Ahab?
options: That there would be no dew or rain except by his word

17.question: How was Elijah fed by the brook Cherith?
options: By ravens, which brought him bread and meat

17.question: What miracle happened at the house of the widow of Zarephath?
options: The meal and oil did not run out during the drought

17.question: What happened to the son of the widow of Zarephath?
options: He was taken to the king

17.question: What did Elijah do for the widow's dead son?
options: He prayed, and the Lord raised him to life

18.title: 'How long halt ye between two opinions?'
18.text: On Carmel, Elijah challenged the people: 'How long halt ye between two opinions? If the Lord be God, follow him; but if Baal, then him.' It was time to decide.

18.question: Whom did Elijah meet before Ahab, who had hidden prophets of the Lord?
What did Ahab call Elijah when he met him?
What challenge did Elijah propose on Mount Carmel?
options: To see which God would answer by fire: the Lord or Baal

18.question: How many prophets of Baal did Elijah face alone?
What did the prophets of Baal do, in vain, to make their god answer?
options: They cried out and cut themselves, but there was no answer

18.question: What did Elijah do to his altar before praying?
options: He soaked it with water three times

18.question: What happened when Elijah prayed to the Lord?
options: Fire fell from heaven and consumed everything

18.question: What did the people cry out when they saw the fire from heaven?
options: 'The Lord, he is God! The Lord, he is God!'

18.question: What happened to the prophets of Baal after the challenge?
What did Elijah announce after the challenge ended?
options: The return of the rain, after praying seven times

19.title: Seven thousand who had not bowed
19.text: Elijah thought he was alone, but God revealed there were 7,000 in Israel who had not bowed their knees to Baal — he was not alone in faithfulness.

19.question: What led Elijah to flee to the wilderness, discouraged?
How was Elijah sustained on the way to Horeb?
Where was the Lord, and not in the wind, earthquake or fire?
Whom did Elijah call to be his successor, finding him plowing?

20.title: God of hills and valleys
20.text: The Syrians said Israel's God was 'a god of the hills,' not of the valleys. God gave the victory in the valley precisely to show that he reigns over all.

20.question: Who besieged Samaria, demanding Ahab's surrender?
What did a prophet announce to Ahab about the battle?
options: That the Lord would deliver the Syrians into his hand

20.question: What was the result of the first clash with Syria?
What did the Syrians say about the God of Israel?
options: That he was 'a god of the hills,' not of the valleys
That he was the same as Baal

20.question: What happened in the second battle, fought in the valley?
options: Israel won again, showing God's power

20.question: What did Ahab do with Ben-hadad on defeating him?
options: He spared him and made a covenant

20.question: What did a prophet do to rebuke Ahab?
options: He pretended to be wounded and led Ahab to judge himself

20.question: What did the prophet announce to Ahab for sparing Ben-hadad?
options: That his life would answer for the king he had released

21.title: Naboth's inheritance
21.text: Naboth refused to sell the vineyard because it was his fathers' inheritance, sacred under Israel's Law. For this Jezebel had him killed — a crime of greed and injustice.

21.question: What did Ahab covet from Naboth?
Why did Naboth refuse to sell the vineyard to Ahab?
What did Jezebel plot so Ahab could have the vineyard?
options: She falsely accused Naboth and had him stoned

21.question: What did Elijah say to Ahab about the crime?
options: 'Hast thou killed, and also taken possession?'

21.question: What judgment did Elijah announce on Ahab's house?
options: That it would be destroyed, and dogs would eat Jezebel
That he would have many children

21.question: What happened when Ahab humbled himself before God?
options: God delayed the punishment to his son's days

22.title: Micaiah, alone against 400
22.text: While 400 prophets flattered Ahab, only Micaiah dared to tell the uncomfortable truth — an example of faithfulness that cost dearly, but came to pass.

22.question: What did Jehoshaphat, king of Judah, propose to Ahab?
What did Ahab's 400 prophets say about the battle?
options: To go up, for he would win

22.question: Whom did Jehoshaphat ask to consult, a prophet of the Lord?
What did Micaiah truly prophesy?
options: Defeat: 'I saw all Israel scattered upon the hills'

22.question: How did Micaiah explain the false prophets?
options: A lying spirit in their mouths, to deceive Ahab

22.question: What did Ahab do with Micaiah over the prophecy?
What did Ahab do to try to escape the prophecy in battle?
options: He disguised himself as an ordinary soldier

22.question: How did Ahab die, despite the disguise?
options: A randomly shot arrow struck him

22.question: What happened to Ahab's blood, fulfilling the prophecy?
What was Jehoshaphat's reign like in Judah?
options: He walked in the right ways, like Asa his father

22.question: Who reigned in Israel after Ahab's death?
options: Ahaziah, his son, who did evil
